tip.kinswathe.judge.ability_failed: Target seems not to be present...
announcement.goals.kinswathe.licensed_vi: You might still retain a shred of conscience...
game.win.kinswathe.licensed_villain: The licensed villain ended everyone's life!
item.kinswathe.icon_power_restoration.to:
Instantly end blackout effect
Activated instantly on purchase, 3 minutes cooldown
item.kinswathe.capture_device.tooltip:
Right-click to place down on floors
Trap passing player and inform the user
Capture Device will automatically disappear after a period of time
Invisible to other players, both placed and in hand
1 minute cooldown
item.kinswathe.wrench.tooltip:
Use on any jammed or pried door to restore to normal
2 minutes cooldown
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:bod:
Bodies are easy to deceive, and the Bodymaker specializes in forging them!

Whether it's cause of death or identity, the Bodymaker can freely fabricate details on their creations, surely leaving investigators deeply perplexed.
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:jud:
The Judge boarded this train purely for vacation, but his innate sense of justice prevents him from turning a blind eye to evil.

The Judge can use his Judgment ability to make a target emit light.

Some say those marked by the Judge will eventually face trial, but others worry whether the Judge's verdicts are truly just...
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:lic:
The Licensed Villain is a rare character holding an officially certified villain license. He only appears when there are enough players.

The Licensed Villain's win condition is unique — he must kill everyone. When all civilians or all killers are eliminated, the game does not end. Only he can end it all with his own hands.

The Licensed Villain has 15 seconds of sprint time, boards with Lockpick, and receives coins for completing tasks.

Revolver can be purchased at the shop, and he suffers no misfire penalty when using it — after all, he's a professional.
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:tec:
The Technician is responsible for the maintenance of the entire train.

Every time the Technician completes a task, they receive 50 coins. They can purchase a Wrench to repair jammed or pried doors.

Additionally, they can purchase a Capture Device to check for suspicious passengers passing by.

During the blackout, the Technician can quickly restore the power system, catching the killers off guard!
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:mag:
The Magnate is the wealthiest person on the train, his pockets always full of endless coins.

The Magnate's passive income is twice that of other characters.
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:tas:
Hard work pays off — the Taskmaster's income is tied to tasks.

If the Taskmaster is on the killer team, completing a task rewards 50 coins.

If on the non-killer team, completing a task grants an additional 25 coins on top of the regular reward.
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:vio: The Violator has broken the most fundamental rule on the train — jumping.
